name: 50 MG Acarbose Tablets IP
about: 50 MG Acarbose Tablets IP is a medication used for the treatment of type 2 diabetes. It is a type of alpha-glucosidase inhibitor that works by slowing down the digestion of carbohydrates in the body, which helps to regulate blood sugar levels. These tablets come in a solid dosage form and physical form of tablets, containing 50 mg of the drug. The medicine originates from India and is classified as a general medicine. These tablets should be stored in a dry place to maintain their effectiveness. They are available for purchase from a variety of businesses, including exporters, suppliers, and traders.

faq.q: What is the recommended dosage for 50 MG Acarbose Tablets IP?
faq.a: The recommended dosage of 50 MG Acarbose Tablets IP is one tablet taken orally three times a day, with the first bite of each meal.

faq.q: How does 50 MG Acarbose Tablets IP work?
faq.a: 50 MG Acarbose Tablets IP is an alpha-glucosidase inhibitor that slows down the digestion of carbohydrates in the body. This helps to regulate blood sugar levels in people with type 2 diabetes.

faq.q: Can 50 MG Acarbose Tablets IP be taken by pregnant women?
faq.a: It is not recommended to take 50 MG Acarbose Tablets IP if you are pregnant or planning to become pregnant. Please consult your doctor before taking this medication.

faq.q: What are the possible side effects of 50 MG Acarbose Tablets IP?
faq.a: The common side effects of 50 MG Acarbose Tablets IP include bloating, gas, diarrhea, and stomach pain. If you experience any severe side effects, please consult your doctor immediately.

faq.q: Is a prescription required to purchase 50 MG Acarbose Tablets IP?
faq.a: Yes, a prescription is required to purchase 50 MG Acarbose Tablets IP. Please consult your doctor before taking this medication.
